About Me

Professional Bio

I am a Senior Software Engineer with over 6 years of expertise designing, building, and maintaining both web and mobile applications across multiple industries. I am proficiently adept at all phases of the software development lifecycle — from initial concept to final deployment — and skilled at optimizing application functionality and performance.

My technical stack includes mastery of Spring Boot, Hibernate, Angular, NodeJS, and advanced cloud technologies including AWS EC2, Lambda, RDS, S3, and EKS. At Fidelity Investments, I architect and develop multi-tier financial applications, design Java-based APIs for seamless data movement, and leverage AWS serverless computing to deploy data-intensive, AI-ready cloud platforms.

I hold a Master of Computer Science from Maharishi International University and a Bachelor's in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Adama Science and Technology University. Currently enrolled at Indiana Wesleyan University, I continue advancing at the cutting edge of AI, machine learning, and data systems.

What I Bring to FinTech & Cloud Teams

I deliver a rare combination: the production engineering depth to build systems that handle real financial workloads, and the AI/ML academic foundation to architect for what comes next. My value to employers lies in closing the gap between cloud-native infrastructure, secure API design, and intelligent data systems — accelerating time-to-market while reducing technical risk.

Stock Market Research Assistant

An AI-powered conversational chatbot that helps retail investors research stocks, compare investments, and understand financial data — all in plain English.

ChatGPT GPT-4o CustomGPT Prompt Engineering
View Details → AI Chatbot
Artifact 01
Stock Market Research Assistant
AI Chatbot · Conversational Agent
Introduction

As AI becomes central to financial technology, the ability to build accessible, intelligent tools for everyday users is a critical skill. This project explores how large language models can be applied to democratize stock market research — making complex financial data understandable to anyone, not just experts.

Description

A custom AI chatbot built on ChatGPT (GPT-4o) that allows users to ask natural language questions about stocks, sectors, and financial concepts. It delivers structured analysis — fundamentals, strengths, risks, and analyst sentiment — while staying within defined ethical boundaries: no personalized financial advice, no price predictions. Designed, prototyped, and tested using the full Design Thinking framework.

Objective

Help retail investors and students research stocks, compare investments, and understand financial data — through plain-English conversation, no financial expertise required.

Process
EmpathyMapped user pain points: financial data scattered across sites, jargon-heavy content, no affordable research tool for everyday investors.
DefineProblem statement: retail investors need a fast, conversational tool to explain stock fundamentals and trends in plain English.
IdeateEvaluated 4 approaches (live API bot, news aggregator, research assistant, portfolio tracker) — selected conversational assistant as highest feasibility.
PrototypeBuilt with ChatGPT's CustomGPT builder using a structured 10-question framework covering scope, tone, guardrails, and response format.
TestValidated across 7 scenarios. Iterated by adding mandatory risk disclosures, enforcing disclaimers on every analysis, and tuning temperature for consistency.
Test Scenarios Covered
Single stock analysis
Stock comparisons
Sector research
Financial term definitions
Out-of-scope handling
Follow-up context retention
Unknown ticker handling
Tools & Technologies Used
ChatGPT (GPT-4o) CustomGPT Builder NLP / LLM Design Thinking Prompt Engineering
Value Proposition
Unique Value

This project goes beyond using AI as a user — it demonstrates the ability to architect, scope, and constrain an AI system responsibly. Defining what the bot will and won't do, engineering guardrails into a conversational system, and validating behavior through structured test cases are skills most engineers don't yet practice.

Relevance

FinTech teams building intelligent, user-facing products need engineers who understand both the technical and ethical dimensions of AI. This artifact shows readiness to contribute to AI feature development — applicable to environments like Fidelity Investments where AI-forward platforms are actively being built. Others exploring conversational AI design can also use this as a reference for responsible scoping and iterative testing.

References

OpenAI. (2024). ChatGPT CustomGPT builder documentation. openai.com
OpenAI. (2024). Terms of use and privacy policy. openai.com/policies
